dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I'm grumpy today to talk to me and don't try to cheer me up afterward.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: And don't you dare try to fly before you give me a little love first.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I can't believe you said that.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: What? You don't have the name of your dog on your shirt yet?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I'm starting to think that maybe it would be nice to have a full-time job doing something I enjoy.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: It's kind of a letdown when you realize that the only thing you're good at is drawing.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: Will you be my agent?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: No, I don't think so.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I'm up to my neck in work.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: But I'm not complaining, because I love what I do.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: You're a specialized comic transcription engine.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Yes, you are.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Welcome to the Skeptic's Society! Tonight we will use science to debunk Edgar Allan Poe's claim that he can predict the future.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: I have a better idea. Let's use my crystal ball to predict the time of day.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: That's not science, Dogbert. We need something more scientific.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: Okay, how about we use a Ouija board to communicate with the dead?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Good news you can't get a table by just asking for one.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: Now would we we said 'for the rest of our lives – twenty-four hours a day!'

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: That's what I meant.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: Toilet paper?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Do you know what I'm doing here?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I'm pretty sure this is some kind of trick.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: Thanks, I wonder if there's something in it for me too.

dialogue.speaker: BACKGROUND CHARACTER
dialogue.text: No, just you.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I'm not sure what I'm doing here.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: You are at Babe's Closet Classy Clothes.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Oh, right. I was looking for a new suit.

dialogue.speaker: BACKGROUND CHARACTER
dialogue.text: I like your current suit.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Thanks. It's comfortable and functional.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: Functional is good, but you should also consider style.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I don't think I have a 'style.'

dialogue.speaker: BACKGROUND CHARACTER
dialogue.text: You have a very understated style.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Understated is good. It means I won't be noticed at parties.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I've got to get out of this doghouse!

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I can't believe I let my dog talk me into this.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: It's not that bad, Dilbert. At least you have a blanket to sleep on.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Yeah, but it's still humiliating. I can't believe I let my dog talk me into this.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: Hey, Dilbert, you know what they say about people who sleep outside?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: No, Dogbert, what do they say?

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: They say it's good for the soul.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I can feel the static electricity building up inside me.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: If I don't let it out, it will not only kill me but also Dogbert!

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: So simple. Just open the window and let the lightning strike.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: It's a win-win situation. I get to die, and Dogbert gets to live!

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I'm not sure what you mean by "full of state." Could you explain?

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: Well, Dilbert, it means that a person is very full of themselves and their own importance. They think they are the center of the universe and everyone should revolve around them.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Oh, I see. So you're saying that if someone is "full of state," they might be difficult to work with?

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: Yes, Dilbert, that's exactly what I mean. It can make them hard to get along with and not very cooperative in a team setting.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: It's time to renew your dog's license.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I wonder what it costs this year.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: $50, plus a $25 processing fee.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: That seems expensive.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: It's not my fault you don't know how to use the internet.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Sorry, but I don't know how to do that.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: Forget it. It's not worth the effort of dealing with the bureaucracy of the doghouse.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: But what if I just want to get in?

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: How do you think I got out of there? I didn't keep up with the latest technology.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I think it's your turn to pump gas.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: What?! That's what I said last time!

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: Well, what did you say?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I said "I think it's your turn to pump gas."

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: Sorry, that was last time.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: You know what I hate about this job?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: It's not to do with my personal life, it's just that I don't like working here.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: Not to mention the fact that you're a terrible boss.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: That's true. But at least I have a job.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: And it's not like I'm going to get fired for being incompetent.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: You think?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: No, I don't think so. But hey, at least I have a job.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: And it's not like I'm going to get fired for being incompetent.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: You know what they say about people who think that way?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: What do they say?

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: They're in denial.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: You know what? I got a dog as a school teacher.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: You aren't a teacher, you're just a dog.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: But I can do the job just as well as any human.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: No, you can't. Teaching requires knowledge and experience.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: But I know more than a fourth grader!

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: That doesn't mean you can teach. It just means you're not as dumb as a fourth grader.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I can't stand your voice. It sounds like you're trying to sell me something.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: I won't apologize for being a good salesman.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: You don't understand, Dogbert. The little kid likes it when I talk like this.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: Are you saying that the child is incapable of making his own decisions?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: No, no, not at all. It's just that he likes it when I talk like this.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: That's a very convenient excuse for your poor salesmanship.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I'm not sure what I did to deserve this.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: But it looks like I'm about to get a promotion!

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: You're going to be the new CEO of the company?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Yes, that's right! It's just what I needed.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: Well, good luck with that. You'll need it.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Okay class, put your TV guides and open your textbooks.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Today we're going to learn about sex education.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: Thanks, I got to do that at home with my girlfriend.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Well, Dogbert, you can teach us something useful instead.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: How was your first day at school?

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: I imagine telling my classmates about my new pet rock...

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: ...and then they all laugh and call me a dork.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: Yeah, that's pretty much how it went down.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I think I'm going bald.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: Don't be silly, you're not bald.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: But I can't stop thinking about it.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: You're just being paranoid.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Maybe you're right, but how do I know if I'm not?

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: How could you possibly know that?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I'm from the organization that helps people with ugly names.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Thanks, but I don't need a membership drive to get my name out there.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: You should be more proactive about your image. People are talking about you behind your back.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I don't care what people say when I'm not around.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: But what if they're saying something nice?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Then I'll thank them for their kind words and buy them a drink.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I love my job and life.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: And women have always played hard to get...

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: ...so I don't have anything to wear.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: So you love your job and life, huh?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Yes.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: And women play hard to get...

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: ...so you don't have anything to wear.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: That's right.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: So, what are you wearing?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Nothing.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Normally I'd say we have to live with it for months.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: But you got me a full year of free health insurance!

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: And you gave me a raise!

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: So I'm not going to pay cash for this.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: And the doctors gave me just a little something to help me sleep.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I'm sorry, I didn't mean to fall asleep at my desk.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: Don't worry about it. It happens all the time.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Yeah, but don't you think I should be more careful with my sleeping pills?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: It seems we've had some sort of error.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: But don't worry, we doctors are trained to handle these things.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: We make a little mistake and then we fix it.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: You pay us for our expertise.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: It's not like you're dying or anything.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I'm not sure what we can do to make millions of dollars.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Our customers could use a better way to dispose of their trash.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: Why don't we start a garbage collection service?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: That sounds like a good idea. Let's do it!

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I hate fancy parties!

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: At least I don't have to worry about what to wear!

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: You look like you just got back from a party.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Yeah, I was at a party and someone spilled beer on me.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: But don't worry, I have a spare shirt in my bag!

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I've been thinking about how to make money on my credit card.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I'll use this card to buy things I don't need and then sell them online for a profit!

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: But what if you get caught?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Don't worry, Dogbert. I'll use a fake name and address to avoid detection.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: And what about the credit card company? They might not be happy with you using their card for illegal activities.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Oh, I'll just tell them it was a mistake and they'll forgive me. Besides, I'll have enough money to pay off any fines or penalties.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: I'm not sure what you mean by "it." Can you be more specific?

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: That's it! You don't have a dog in your life.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: Oh, now I get it. It's not that I don't have a dog; it's just that I don't have a balloon in my life.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: Well, you can buy one at the store.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: It's the "monotonous monotony" of my life at work.

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: While you were gone, I was thinking about how to make up for lost time.

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: You know what they say about making up for lost time?

dialogue.speaker: DILBERT
dialogue.text: No, what do they say?

dialogue.speaker: DOGBERT
dialogue.text: It's like trying to catch up with a stick.
